summary: The schema docs channel keeps one `description` per property, so every guided clause but the last silently drops its teaching — eight lost today; give it the validation channel's accumulation

edit.description: The docs-channel loop — the second `for clause in &contract.clauses` (169-186, after the validation match) — `insert`s `description` into the shared property map once per guided clause, and `insert` is last-writer-wins: on a field carrying N guided clauses, N-1 teachings leave no trace and nothing reports the loss. **The accumulation surface already exists one channel over**: `push_subschema` (318) was added by SHAPE-PREDICATE (0927979) for this exact hazard on `pattern`, and its own doc comment states the rule — 'writing a second `pattern` straight onto the property would leave only the last clause's standing — silently dropping the other from the schema, with nothing to catch it'. This is that hazard's twin in the neighbouring loop, so extend that home's rule rather than minting a second composition story: join the field's guided clauses into the one `description` in clause order (deterministic — `contract.clauses` is the compiled order), so adding a clause adds prose instead of re-picking which teaching an author sees. Joining is what the corpus already demands (`specs/builtins.md`, 'The clauses live in code': guidance 'rides the clause value itself, so it cannot dangle from the check it explains'); the capture's pick-a-winner alternative needs a stated rule for which clause wins, and there is none — author order decides by accident. `Predicate::documented_field` (`src/contract.rs`) is the gate on which predicates write here at all, and it is why `mention-reachable` contributes nothing. All addresses re-read on disk at a2e48aa.

tests.asserts: a field carrying several guided clauses reaches hover with every teaching present, in clause order; a single guided clause still emits its prose unadorned; a field-less predicate's guidance still rides no channel

acceptance: Every guided clause on a field reaches its property's `description` — `skill.name`'s six and `skill.description`'s four each keep all their teaching, so the 1024-cap prose an author needs is back at the keystroke. The three existing channel tests in `src/schema.rs`'s `mod tests` (623, 667, 699) still hold. `cargo test` green.
notes: Drained from `.flume/refactor/build-schema-docs-channel-single-slot.md` (filed by SHAPE-PREDICATE, observed 94012c4); capture deleted. Verified at a2e48aa via `temper schema --kind skill`: only the last clause's guidance survives — `description` carries the shape teaching, the 1024-cap prose gone; `name` carries `deny`'s. Live loss is 8 (the verb models skill+rule alone); 14 more latent in agent/command/marketplace/plugin-manifest. `rule.paths` is unaffected. scoped at a2e48aa.

summary: Ship the temper-owned `dial` kind: a local-locus document naming clauses by label and declaring the severity this machine reads them at — deletion unspellable, softening inert in block mode

edit.description: `all_kinds` (374-388) ships eleven claude-code kinds; the dial joins as temper's own — its schema IS 0030's envelope: an entry names a clause by its compiled label and declares a severity, and the schema cannot spell deletion, so a dialed clause still reports. The file is untouched across 0c3cbcb..a2e48aa, so this address carries from last tick's re-read; the roster still carries eleven.

The provider face. The dial is **temper-owned, not a claude-code kind**, so it exports from the root, never the `claude-code` subpath (`specs/builtins.md`: the shipped claude-code kinds export from that subpath alone). **Cite stale by one ship**: 0927979 edited this file (+24 — the skill contract's two `shape` clauses, and the marketplace hold header's rewrite), so its line addresses re-stamp at next tick's audit before this entry is picked.

The root export carries the dial. The predicate-ctor export block is where the window's churn lands: `closedKeys` joined at 29 (7fae62e) and `shape` at 50 (0927979), both re-read on disk at a2e48aa — so the dial's export lands beside a block two consecutive ships have moved, and this is the file that serialized this entry behind SHAPE-PREDICATE only via `sdk/src/builtins.ts`.

The dial's document face is ruled (0034): a local-locus `toml-document`. **Every half is upstream code on disk by the time this is picked** — `Commitment::Local` (46-56) and `CustomKind::local` (735) shipped at 40619a0, the layout-only arm that would otherwise refuse this kind on contact is retired (bce89b7, `local_locus_fault` at 750 now fencing the `governs`-less locus alone), `Format::TomlDocument` (568, its name at 580 and its decode at 975) shipped at 09ef5ea as a read face with no write twin — exactly what a never-projected local document needs — and 6e7b958 made the walk find the dial's own `.temper/dial.toml` at all, which is what keeps this kind's rows from deriving over an empty set. The file is untouched across 0c3cbcb..a2e48aa, so every address carries from last tick's re-read. This entry declares the kind; it mints no format and no locus mechanics of its own.

Check applies the dial's severities to the labelled clauses it names, and its softening is inert in block mode — so a block-mode pass on any machine implies the shared gate's pass. b753358 reshaped this file around the lock family, and the dial rides that shape rather than minting a second one: `assemble_lock_family` (1457) is the one read of every layered input, before any consumer, and returns the `LockFamily` (1426-1436) whose `joined_clauses` field is where a policy layer's clauses already sit. Read it before adding a severity-application seam. The file is untouched across 0c3cbcb..a2e48aa, so these addresses carry from last tick's re-read.

schemaDelta: none of the lock's own — the dial is a shipped kind declaring a local locus over the `toml-document` format, and a local member's rows never enter the lock, deriving at read time instead.

tests.asserts: a dial entry re-reads a clause at the declared severity and the clause still reports; a dial cannot spell deletion; dialed softening is inert in block mode while dialed hardening binds in every mode

acceptance: See the annoying finding, read its label, dial it — locally, uncommitted, visibly, and without ever softening the shared gate (contract.md, "clause"; pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: **Gate opened this tick**: SHAPE-PREDICATE shipped (0927979 — `Predicate::Shape` at `contract.rs`:241, `tests/shape_predicate.rs` on disk), so the `sdk/src/builtins.ts` serialization holding this entry is discharged. Un-forked at 832f015: 0034 ruled `(local-locus-toml-face)` whole. All three 0034 derivations ship (bce89b7, 09ef5ea, 6e7b958) — nothing upstream is unbuilt. The two SDK cites above are stale by that ship and re-stamp at next tick's audit. scoped f1d97e4.

summary: `check` announces every active local member, every dialed clause, and every joined lock — the gate says out loud which inputs judged this run

edit.description: The check run announces the three uncommitted-or-joined inputs by name: each active local member, each dialed clause (by its label), each joined lock. The announcement is unconditional — a run judged by inputs review never saw says so, whatever the verdict. Two of the three are already assembled and need no re-derivation: `assemble_lock_family` (1457) returns the `LockFamily` (1426-1436) carrying both the local-locus read-time rows and `joined_clauses`, read once before any consumer. The joined third is the live gap b753358 named and deferred here by name: it shipped the join with every finding carrying its layer in the address (`<label>@<layer>`, `qualify_layer_label` at 1546), so a layer that produces no finding gates silently today — exactly what this closes. The file is untouched across 0c3cbcb..a2e48aa, so these addresses carry from last tick's re-read.

Every reporter renders one diagnostic source (`specs/distribution.md`): `session_start` (65), `github` (141), `sarif` (172) each carry the announcement in their own shape rather than one reporter growing a private line. The file is untouched across 0c3cbcb..a2e48aa, so all three addresses carry from last tick's re-read.

tests.asserts: a check run with a local member, a dialed clause, and a joined lock announces all three; a run with none announces nothing extra

acceptance: `check` announces every active local member, every dialed clause, and every joined lock (pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` green.
notes: Last of 0032's five — it announces what the other four introduce, so it is honest only once they exist. The joined-lock third is **built and unannounced** (b753358), the first of the three to reach that state; scope is unchanged, and the commit body should say so. Serialized behind the dial (shares `src/main.rs`). scoped f1d97e4; cites carry from 0c3cbcb.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (59) sits under a doc (55-58) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (621) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (648), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 519 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 618-620 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 643-646 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth'). The file is untouched across 0c3cbcb..a2e48aa, so every address carries from last tick's re-read.

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1349) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1350-1354) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1352) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number. The file is untouched across 0c3cbcb..a2e48aa, so the addresses carry from last tick's re-read.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at 0c3cbcb and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics. The window touched neither file, so every address carries from last tick's re-read rather than being re-derived. The constant is still 5 on disk (`graph.rs`:59), so the park's subject is untouched — and may still be wrong. rescoped 4a33911; re-cited 53519b9; park re-tested 0c3cbcb.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at 0c3cbcb, holding on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff 385c429..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.
